items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
`ZYNQ.peer` is a lightweight WebRTC wrapper built on top of PeerJS. It simplifies real-time peer-to-peer communication (messaging, audio, video) with minimal setup.

## Features
- Peer-to-peer messaging (1-to-1 + broadcast)
- Audio & video calls
- Built-in invite / accept flow
- Heartbeat + timeout system
- Automatic stream management (reuse + cleanup)
- Bitrate monitoring (video only)
- Event-driven API
- No backend required

items.label: Quick Start
items.content:
## Setup
```javascript
import 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.2.5/index.min.js'
await ZYNQ.load("peer")
await ZYNQ.peer.start() // optional custom ID
```

## Get your ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('ready', id => {
  console.log('My ID:', id)
})
```

## Connect to a peer
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect('peer-id')
```

## Send a message
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo('peer-id', 'Hello')
```

## Receive messages
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {
  console.log(peerId, message)
})
```

items.label: Start
items.content:
## Start peer
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.start(customId?)
```
- Optional custom ID
- Connects to broker
- Emits `ready`

items.label: Destroy
items.content:
## Destroy
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.destroy()
```
- Closes all connections
- Stops media devices
- Clears internal state
- Emits `destroyed`

items.label: Events
items.content:
## Listen
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('event', callback)
```

## Event list

| Event | Data | Purpose |
|------|------|--------|
| `ready` | id | Your peer ID |
| `invite` | {id, config, accept, reject} | Incoming connection |
| `joined` | peerId | Connected |
| `left` | peerId | Disconnected |
| `message` | {peerId, message} | Messaging |
| `local-stream` | MediaStream | Local preview |
| `remote-stream` | {peerId, stream} | Remote media |
| `bitrate` | {peerId, kbps} | Connection quality |
| `rejected` | peerId | Call rejected |
| `error` | {code, peerId?, message} | Errors |
| `destroyed` | - | Cleanup |

## Notes
- `invite` → call `accept()` or `reject()`
- `remote-stream` → handle incoming video/audio
- `bitrate` → fires ~every 2s when video is active

items.label: Messaging
items.content:
## Send to one
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo(peerId, message)
```

## Broadcast
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.broadcast(message)
```

## Receive
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {})
```

`message` supports any type (string, object, array, etc.)

items.label: Audio & Video
Connections
items.content:
## Disconnect
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.disconnect(peerId)
```

## Get your ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getMyId()
```

## Active peers
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getActivePeers()
```
Returns an array of peer IDs

items.label: System & Advanced
items.content:
## Behavior
- Heartbeat: every 2s
- Timeout: 12s inactivity
- Stream reuse + auto cleanup
- Bitrate only when video is active

## Debug
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setDebug(2)
```
0 = off  
1 = normal  
2+ = verbose

## Disable auto-start
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setAutoStart(false)
```
